nodes.label: Unit tests for runner-side WebSocket tunnel serving helpers.

Protocol close codes are extracted for fail-loud retry decisions.      :returns:
Exceptions without close metadata do not look fatal.      :returns: None.
HTTP upgrade rejections expose their status for retry policy.      :returns: Non
A clean server-side close still sleeps before reconnecting.      :param monkeypa
A later successful connection resets accumulated retry backoff.      :param monk
Permanent server close codes are not retried.      :param monkeypatch: Pytest mo
HTTP 401/403 during WS upgrade stops the reconnect loop.      :param monkeypatch
An ``InvalidURI`` carrying an https:// target is classified as auth.      Mirror
A bare malformed ``ws://`` URL is NOT an auth redirect.      A literal ``ws://``
Non-``InvalidURI`` exceptions return ``None`` (no false fatals).      The helper
A WS upgrade redirected to an OAuth login URL exits immediately.      Previously
Authenticated remote tunnels pass the bearer on the WS handshake.      :param mo
A recorded ?o= selector rides the tunnel handshake.      The WS upgrade must nam
Malformed frames must be logged and skipped, not raised.      :param malformed:
A valid request following a malformed frame still dispatches.      :returns: Non
HTTP request frames reset the runner idle timer.      :returns: None.
Tunnel pings are keepalives and must not prevent idle shutdown.      :returns: N
Tunneled WebSocket channel frames reset the idle timer.      :returns: None.
Request-cancel frames reset the idle timer.      :returns: None.
The token factory is called before each connection attempt.      :param monkeypa
HTTP 401 triggers a factory refresh and retries immediately.      :param monkeyp
HTTP 401 without a factory remains fatal (existing behavior).      :param monkey
HTTP 403 stays fatal even when a factory is available.      :param monkeypatch:
After a disconnect, reconnect sends the REFRESHED token, not     the initial one
